title: [Handshake] Support for tuple type
body:
Currently, a handshake function with multiple arguments introduces handshaking for each argument separately. In some cases this is not required, as the input arrives in the form of a tuple.

The main problem with supporting MLIR's builtin `TupleType` (https://mlir.llvm.org/docs/Dialects/Builtin/#tupletype) is that it does not come with standard operations to operate on such types (Rational link: https://mlir.llvm.org/docs/Dialects/Builtin/#tupletype). Therefore, we would have to introduce tuple access and packing operations. One potential use of these additions would look as follows (operation names to be defined):

```
handshake.func @types(%in: tuple<i64, i32, i64>, %arg1: none, ...) -> (tuple<i32, i64>, none) attributes {argNames = ["in0", "inCtrl"], resNames = ["out0", "outCtrl"]} {
  %a, %b, %c = handshake.unpack %in : (tuple<i64,i32,i64>) -> (i64,i32,i64)
  %sum = arith.addi %a, %c : i64

  %res = handshake.pack %b, %sum : (i32, i64) -> tuple<i32, i64>

  return %res, %arg1 : tuple<i32, i64>, none
}
```

Do you guys think that such additions to handshake are reasonable?
